title: The Third Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## June 13, 2021 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Third Sunday after Pentecost falls on June 13, 2021, placing the sermon within Ordinary Time following the Easter season. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday would include 1 Samuel 15:34-16:13 (David's anointing), Psalm 20, 2 Corinthians 5:6-10 (14-17), and Mark 4:26-34 (parables of the kingdom). This falls in the liturgical season of sustained teaching through parables and kingdom theology in the Gospel of Mark.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention planning (the triennial gathering meets in July 2021, less than a month away). Broderick Greer, a prominent Black Episcopal priest and activist, was serving as rector of Saint John's Cathedral during a period of heightened denominational focus on racial justice following General Convention resolutions and statements made in 2018 and anticipated discussions in 2021. The Diocese of Colorado would have been actively engaged in post-pandemic reopening protocols for in-person worship and community gatherings.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing full reopening after COVID-19 restrictions with indoor dining and gathering capacity limits being lifted in mid-June 2021. The Capitol Hill neighborhood surrounding Saint John's Cathedral was navigating increased homelessness and safety concerns that had emerged during the pandemic, with ongoing debates about public space usage. Colorado was in the midst of summer weather, with temperatures in the 70s-80s Fahrenheit during early-mid June.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The nation was focused on the January 6 Capitol riot investigation, with the House Select Committee newly formed and beginning its work in mid-June 2021. President Biden was preparing for his first international trip (to Europe, departing June 9), meeting with allies to reaffirm NATO commitments and confront Russian actions. COVID-19 vaccination rates were accelerating with the Delta variant emerging as a growing concern, and mask mandates were being lifted nationwide despite epidemiological warnings.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Israeli-Palestinian conflict had recently escalated dramatically, with a ceasefire reached on May 20 after 11 days of intense fighting in Gaza, leaving widespread destruction and humanitarian concerns. Putin's Russia was being scrutinized internationally following the Biden-Putin summit being scheduled for June 16 in Geneva, just three days after this sermon. Brazil was experiencing severe flooding and mudslides in Petropolis, Rio de Janeiro state, killing over 200 people in late May/early June.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was experiencing a complex moment of pandemic recovery mixed with political polarization, racial reckoning, and vaccine hesitancy debates. The spiritual and institutional health of mainline Protestant churches including the Episcopal Church was undergoing scrutiny regarding their response to racial justice movements and their ability to retain membership post-pandemic. Sports culture was normalizing with fans returning to stadiums, and the NBA Finals were underway (Phoenix Suns vs. Milwaukee Bucks), while the Euro 2020 soccer championship was beginning in Europe during this same week.
